what_it_does: Defines a dark, phone-screen analytics dashboard page with a hero views metric, donut chart, legend, bar chart, top-piece list, count strip, and bottom rail icons generated through DNA.org helpers.
observations: The file imports Google fonts plus shared piece and phone CSS from /surface/styles.
Inline CSS sets a charcoal-derived dark stage with gold, ochre, bronze, and page-tone accents through CSS custom properties.
The dashboard content is static sample analytics data: 3,275 views, 38 pieces live, 9 seams open, weekly bar values, and a top-pieces list.
JavaScript injects a donut chart, legend rows, bar chart, and rail icons into placeholder elements.
Comments explicitly describe the dashboard archetype as product-verified and built around dark ground, gold data, one hero figure, and small multiples.
standouts: Strongly token-driven visual system using registry colors and DNA spacing/type variables.
Composition centers on one large KPI supported by small analytic modules.
Chart coloration encodes a gold-to-bronze positional ramp.
conventions: Uses a self-contained HTML piece with inline component-specific CSS and small inline initialization script.
Follows the screen/piece structure with .screen.stage, .sbar, .p-hd, .countstrip, and .p-rail.
Uses CSS custom properties for color, spacing, radius, and type instead of hard-coded design values in most places.
Uses DNA.org runtime helpers for repeated visual organisms rather than hand-written SVG markup.
concerns: The page depends on external CSS variables and DNA.org helpers that are not defined in this file.
Inline script assumes the target element IDs and DNA.org methods exist before execution.
The sample analytics values are embedded directly in the HTML and script.
notes: A compact analytics-stage substrate: dark product surface, gold-led KPI hierarchy, and helper-rendered charts/navigation.
questions: What exact dimensions and phone frame behavior are provided by the imported phone.css?
Are the sample metrics placeholders, fixture data, or intended visible content?
What does the referenced product-verified Analytics Portal source define beyond the comment?
Should the bottom rail icons represent navigation state or only visual scaffolding?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated dashboard archetype matches the actual file: it renders a dark analytics portal screen with one hero figure, small multiples, and gold/bronze data treatment.
intention_signals: It seems intended to express an analytics dashboard archetype for the design system, showing how product metrics, chart organisms, color axes, and navigation rail treatment combine on a dark stage.
novelty: The distinct element is the explicit mapping of data proximity or register to a gold-through-bronze ramp on a charcoal product surface.
contribution: A fused system would keep the dark analytics screen substrate, the hero KPI with supporting chart modules, the gold/bronze data-axis treatment, and the pattern of runtime-generated visual organisms inside a tokenized phone layout.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML/CSS/JS phone-screen analytics dashboard piece with a charcoal stage, gold KPI, donut and bar charts, legend, top-piece list, count strip, and DNA.org-generated bottom rail icons.
intention_for_embedding: This was for demonstrating a product-mode dashboard archetype where analytics content is staged through a dark surface, one dominant metric, small multiples, and a gold-to-bronze visual axis.
